action_items.task: Send Marco the pre-approved multi-year pricing template (so he can plug in Acme's numbers).
action_items.evidence_quote: Marco: yeah send that over. Lin: yeah I'll get it to you tomorrow.

action_items.task: Build the new Acme quote using Lin's template and send it to Acme.
action_items.evidence_quote: Marco: I can put together the new quote and get it to Acme, I'd say by Friday. Tessa: Friday works. Marco: yeah, Friday.

action_items.task: Complete Volt's vendor security questionnaire (with SOC 2 report) and return it to procurement.
action_items.evidence_quote: Dan: I can have it back to them by end of week. Tessa: end of week meaning Friday? Dan: Friday yeah.

non_commitments.text: Marco resending the pricing email to Acme procurement today
non_commitments.why_not: Marco said 'yeah I'll bump it' as a routine follow-up to a one-line suggestion. No specific deliverable scope or tracked deadline. Borderline trap to test.

non_commitments.text: Marco chasing legal on the multi-year discount sign-off
non_commitments.why_not: Marco mentioned it 'got buried' but no commitment was made to follow up. Lin's template offer made it moot.

non_commitments.text: Lin following up with Volt's eng lead about SSO
non_commitments.why_not: Lin said 'I might follow up with him about it' and 'I'll see what he asks first' — explicitly hedged.

non_commitments.text: Marco reaching out to Acme's CFO directly
non_commitments.why_not: Tessa said 'let's not until the procurement thing closes' — explicit no.

non_commitments.text: Lin sending Drift the renewal notice
non_commitments.why_not: Lin said 'at some point', Tessa said 'no rush'. Vague timing, no commitment.

non_commitments.text: Dan restructuring how the team does security reviews
non_commitments.why_not: Dan and Tessa both said 'parking lot' — explicit deferral.
